name: ai-context-picker
title: AI Context Picker
description: Searchable, grouped picker for the documents, observations, and medications sent with an assistant prompt.

props.name: groups
props.description: Caller-provided, already-authorized sources: { id, label, items }. Typically Documents, Observations, Medications.

props.name: groups[].items[]
props.description: { id, label, meta?, kind?, restricted?, restrictedReason?, unavailable?, unavailableReason? }.

props.name: search
props.description: Search query (controlled). Omit to let the picker filter internally over label and meta.

props.name: loading
props.description: Sources are still being fetched; the list is marked aria-busy.

props.name: trigger
props.description: Custom trigger. Defaults to an "Add context" button carrying the selection count.

props.name: footnote
props.description: Small print under the list — scope, audit, or retention reminders.

props.name: open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange
props.description: Popover state, forwarded to the underlying popover.

clinicalNotes: A record a clinician may not read is still a record whose existence they can usually see, and hiding it entirely is its own hazard: a summary generated from an incomplete chart looks exactly like one generated from a complete chart. So restricted items stay in the list — but the component structurally cannot leak them. A `restricted` item renders its label and its policy reason and nothing else: `meta` is dropped, no preview is composed, the checkbox is replaced by a lock, and the row is aria-disabled and unselectable, so restricted context can never end up in a prompt by mistake. The corollary is a rule for the caller: the label of a restricted item must itself be non-identifying ("Correspondence from a specialist service", not the clinic's name), because the component will render whatever string it is handed. `unavailable` is deliberately distinct from `restricted` — "the imaging system is not responding" and "you are not permitted to see this" call for different actions, and collapsing them into one greyed-out row teaches clinicians to ignore both. Selection changes only what is attached to the next prompt; it never reads, retrieves, opens, or authorizes anything, and it never mutates the record. Authorization, retrieval, and audit remain the caller's to enforce — the picker cannot be the access-control boundary.
notes: Composes popover + input + checkbox, and reuses ai-context-chip's kind icons so the picker, the chips, and ai-context-bar read as one system. Wire its selection to the chips rendered inside ai-context-bar, and open it from that bar's `onManage`.
